text:
Once upon a gentle breeze, in the heart of a lush forest where the sunlight danced through the leaves and painted the ground in golden hues, there lived a small, shy rabbit named Thumper. Thumper was different from other rabbits; he had a special gift of creating tiny flower beds wherever he went. His feet, soft as the clouds, left behind not just footprints but also rows of delicate flowers.

One sunny morning, Thumper decided to venture further into the forest than ever before. As he hopped along, he noticed a clearing ahead. It was unusually quiet, and the sun seemed to shine brighter here. Suddenly, Thumper heard a faint sobbing sound. He followed the noise and found an injured fawn, lying in a shallow pool of light that fell between two trees. The fawn's leg was twisted in an unnatural way, and it looked up at Thumper with big, scared eyes.
